description: An object used for holding or transporting something; a receptacle. 'Molu' names what holds and protects—the space shaped to receive.
sound_symbolism: The word holds gently. 'Mo' grounds with stable presence, foundation set. Then 'lu' curves inward to receive, container ready.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'molu' (the container). General term—'lopha' (vessel), 'wiru' (basket), 'tomi' (pot) are specific types.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Holding without grasping—the container receives without possessing.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Vessels sustained life—storing grain, carrying water, preserving what sustains.
